proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_078 (range 30-90, mean ~65, plausibly age in years) is monotonically associated with pfs_months in the prostate cohort.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; rho, p = stats.spearmanr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho between feature_078 and pfs_months = +0.853, p ~ 0; this is by far the largest |rho| among all features and indicates a strong monotonic POSITIVE association (older feature_078 -> longer pfs_months). Mean pfs_months by feature_078 quintile rises monotonically from 1.35 mo (Q1, ages 30-56.5) to 6.24 mo (Q5, ages 73.5-90).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a univariable linear regression, each one-unit increase in feature_078 is associated with a positive increase in pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS slope = +0.1740 pfs_months per 1-unit increase in feature_078, p ~ 0, R^2 = 0.732. feature_078 alone explains ~73% of variance in pfs_months — strongly positive and dominant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (ordinal with levels 0/1/2; counts ~17.6k/25.0k/7.4k) shows a monotonically decreasing relationship with pfs_months: each higher level corresponds to a shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['pfs_months'].mean(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_057=0 -> 4.68, =1 -> 3.49, =2 -> 2.38. Linear slope = -1.161 pfs_months per level, p ~ 0, R^2 = 0.151. Strongly negative and monotone — consistent with a stage- or treatment-line-like ordinal predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (continuous, very right-skewed, range 0.12-3622, ~12.5k unique values) is negatively associated with pfs_months on a log scale (log10(feature_013) reduces pfs_months).

analyses.code: df['log_f013'] = np.log10(df['feature_013']); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ log_f013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: On the raw scale, beta = -0.00334 per unit, p = 3.5e-260, R^2=0.024. On a log10 scale, beta = -0.801 per log10 unit, p ~ 0, R^2 = 0.053. log10(feature_013) gives a much better fit (more than 2x R^2), confirming the relationship is roughly log-linear and significantly NEGATIVE.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is negatively associated with pfs_months in a univariable model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS slope = -0.0749 pfs_months per 1-unit increase in feature_006, p = 9.9e-226, R^2 = 0.020. Significantly NEGATIVE; modest effect size.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80, plausibly a serum-protein-like lab) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_009 -> longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS slope = +0.4949 pfs_months per 1-unit increase in feature_009, p = 1.5e-166, R^2 = 0.015. Significantly POSITIVE.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary feature_051 (univariable mean pfs_months 4.03 when 0 vs 3.51 when 1) is associated with shorter pfs_months when set; this association will persist after adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, log10(feature_013), feature_006 and feature_009.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051', data=df).fit(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 + feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariable: beta = -0.521, p = 9.8e-182. After adjustment for the five other predictors: adjusted beta = -0.0018, p = 0.645 — the apparent univariable effect of feature_051 is fully accounted for by the other predictors (confounded). Direction-of-effect hypothesis SUPPORTED univariably but REFUTED in adjusted analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Self-reported race/ethnicity feature_018 (asian, black, hispanic, other, white) is associated with pfs_months — specifically, mean pfs_months differs across racial groups in this prostate cohort.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_018)', data=df).fit().compare_f_test(smf.ols('pfs_months ~ 1', data=df).fit())
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: white 3.741, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, asian 3.797, other 3.749. ANOVA F = 0.564, p = 0.689, R^2 < 0.0001. NO evidence of racial disparity in pfs_months in this dataset; hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type feature_045 (medicare, medicaid, private, uninsured) is associated with pfs_months — patients with public/uninsured coverage will have shorter pfs_months than privately insured patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_045)', data=df).fit().compare_f_test(smf.ols('pfs_months ~ 1', data=df).fit())
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA F = 0.344, p = 0.794, R^2 = 2e-5. NO insurance-related disparity in pfs_months; hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (range 6-10 with peak at 7, plausibly a Gleason-grade-like ordinal in a prostate cohort) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_067 -> shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_067', data=df).fit(); df.groupby('feature_067')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_067: 6 -> 3.71, 7 -> 3.76, 8 -> 3.74, 9 -> 3.74, 10 -> 3.70. OLS slope = -0.0062, p = 0.486. NO association between feature_067 and pfs_months — surprising if this corresponds to a Gleason-like grade. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable linear regression for pfs_months on {feature_078, feature_057, log10(feature_013), feature_006, feature_009}, all five predictors retain their univariable signs (positive for feature_078 and feature_009; negative for feature_057, log10(feature_013), and feature_006), and together explain >90% of the variance in pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted estimates: feature_078 +0.1737 (p~0), feature_057 -1.150 (p~0), log_f013 -0.804 (p~0), feature_006 -0.0769 (p~0), feature_009 +0.478 (p~0). All five retain univariable sign and remain highly significant. R^2 = 0.969 — the five predictors jointly explain ~97% of variance in pfs_months. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 is heterogeneous across racial groups feature_018 (i.e., there is a feature_018 x feature_078 interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078*C(feature_018)', data=df).fit().compare_f_test(smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit())
analyses.result_summary: Stratified slopes: asian +0.1752, black +0.1749, white +0.1739, hispanic +0.1738, other +0.1717 — extremely close. F-test for interaction: F = 0.323, p = 0.863. NO heterogeneity by race in the feature_078 effect; hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 differs by insurance type feature_045 (e.g., patients with medicaid/uninsured may have a smaller positive slope than privately/medicare-insured patients).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078*C(feature_045)', data=df).fit().compare_f_test(smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit())
analyses.result_summary: Stratified slopes: medicare +0.1741, private +0.1738, uninsured +0.1737, medicaid +0.1749 — within 0.001 of each other. F-test for interaction: F = 0.194, p = 0.900. NO heterogeneity in feature_078 effect by insurance; hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 and feature_057 interact: the protective slope of feature_078 on pfs_months is attenuated at higher feature_057 levels (negative interaction beta).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_078:feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.0097 per unit-feature_078 per unit-feature_057, p ~ 0 (extremely small). Stratified feature_078 slopes: feature_057=0 -> +0.180, =1 -> +0.174, =2 -> +0.159 — slope decreases with stage/level. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 and log10(feature_013) interact: the negative effect of log10(feature_013) on pfs_months is more pronounced (more negative) at older feature_078 (i.e., negative interaction beta).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_078:log_f013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.0070 per unit-feature_078 per log10(feature_013), p = 3.9e-145. The biomarker effect amplifies modestly with feature_078. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 and log10(feature_013) interact: the negative effect of log10(feature_013) on pfs_months is attenuated (less negative) at higher feature_057 levels (positive interaction beta).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_057:log_f013', data=df).fit(); for s in [0,1,2]: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ log_f013', data=df[df.feature_057==s]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.0473, p = 4.5e-32. Stratified slopes: feature_057=0 -> -0.839, =1 -> -0.800, =2 -> -0.734 — biomarker effect weakens with higher feature_057, consistent with the positive interaction. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 and feature_009 interact positively: the protective slope of feature_009 on pfs_months is greater (more positive) at older feature_078.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_078:feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.0043 per unit-feature_078 per unit-feature_009, p = 5.9e-41. Significantly POSITIVE — consistent with feature_009 being more protective in older patients. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 and feature_009 interact negatively: the positive slope of feature_009 on pfs_months is attenuated (smaller) at higher feature_057 levels.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_057:feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.0258 per unit-feature_057 per unit-feature_009, p = 3.2e-08. Stratified slopes for feature_009: feature_057=0 -> +0.492, =1 -> +0.481, =2 -> +0.438 — protective effect of feature_009 weakens at higher feature_057. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within strata of feature_057, the binary feature_051 has a negative within-stratum effect on pfs_months (i.e., the strong univariable association of feature_051 in iteration 7 is at least partly preserved when feature_057 is held fixed but not other predictors).

analyses.code: for s in [0,1,2]: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051', data=df[df.feature_057==s]).fit(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051*feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_057=0: beta -0.559, p=1e-83; =1: beta -0.514, p=5e-105; =2: beta -0.472, p=5e-31. Conditioning only on feature_057, the negative direction of feature_051 is preserved in every stratum. The feature_051 x feature_057 interaction term beta = +0.0438, p = 0.074 (not significant). The feature_051 association seen here is driven by its correlation with feature_078 (which is the dominant predictor); see iteration 7. Direction-only hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 (race/ethnicity) does not modify the slope of pfs_months on the canonical biomarkers feature_057, log10(feature_013), or feature_009 — i.e., the prognostic associations are homogeneous across racial groups.

analyses.code: for v in ['feature_057','log_f013','feature_009']: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {v}*C(feature_018)', data=df).fit().compare_f_test(smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {v} + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit())
analyses.result_summary: F-tests for feature_018 x predictor interactions: feature_057 F=0.221 p=0.927; log_f013 F=0.530 p=0.714; feature_009 F=2.730 p=0.0275 (marginal). Of these, only feature_009 shows a marginal interaction with race that would not survive conservative multiple-comparison correction across the four predictor x race tests in this and iteration 12. Overall homogeneity hypothesis SUPPORTED for feature_057, log10(feature_013) and feature_078; weak/marginal evidence for heterogeneity of feature_009 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the ~80 candidate binary features in the dataset, two specific features feature_109 and feature_039 show independent positive effects on pfs_months after adjustment for the iteration-11 predictor set {feature_078, feature_057, log10(feature_013), feature_006, feature_009}.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {c} + feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted screen of all ~80 binary features. Top two by adjusted p-value: feature_109 beta = +0.1522, p = 6.6e-181; feature_039 beta = +0.1487, p = 6.1e-176. Both >5x larger in absolute value than any other adjusted binary effect (next is feature_027 at +0.025, p=7.5e-15). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The univariable association of feature_109 with pfs_months is small (mean pfs_months 3.73 when 0 vs 3.83 when 1, ~5000 ones in 50000) but statistically significant and is amplified after adjustment because feature_109 is positively correlated with feature_078 (its own pfs_months-shortening confounder).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109', data=df).fit(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109 + feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariable: beta = +0.1005, p = 8.8e-04. Adjusted: beta = +0.1516, p = 2.5e-182. Effect grows ~50% after adjustment, consistent with masking by negatively-associated covariates. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 has the same qualitative behavior as feature_109: a small univariable positive effect on pfs_months that is enlarged after adjustment for the iteration-11 predictor set.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_039', data=df).fit(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_039 + feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariable: beta = +0.0943, p = 1.6e-03. Adjusted: beta = +0.1481, p = 2.6e-177. Same qualitative pattern as feature_109. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between pfs_months and feature_078 is non-linear (specifically convex/upward-curving), captured by adding a quadratic term in feature_078.

analyses.code: df['age2']=df['feature_078']**2; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + age2', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Quadratic term beta = +7.76e-04, p = 1.9e-112; linear term remains positive +0.0735, p = 1.6e-60. Positive quadratic coefficient indicates a slight upward-curving (convex) increase of pfs_months with feature_078 — pfs_months rises faster at older ages (Q1 mean 1.35, Q5 mean 6.24). Hypothesis SUPPORTED — relationship is monotonically positive with mild upward curvature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a final multivariable model that adjusts for {feature_078, feature_057, log10(feature_013), feature_006, feature_009, feature_109, feature_039}, neither feature_018 (race) nor feature_045 (insurance) explains additional variance in pfs_months — i.e., there is no race or insurance disparity in pfs_months once the clinical predictors are held fixed.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_039 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All race and insurance dummy coefficients are tiny (|beta| <= 0.005) with p>0.5. The seven clinical predictors are all highly significant in the expected direction: feature_078 +0.1738, feature_057 -1.151, log_f013 -0.804, feature_006 -0.0771, feature_009 +0.477, feature_109 +0.152, feature_039 +0.148 (all p ~ 0). Final model R^2 = 0.970. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — no residual race or insurance disparity in pfs_months after clinical adjustment.
